prompt: Draft a detailed wet-lab protocol for expressing and purifying recombinant SARS-CoV-2 spike receptor-binding domain (RBD, residues 319–541) from HEK293F suspension cells for neutralising antibody assays and cryo-EM studies. The protocol should include: (1) cloning strategy: RBD with N-terminal secretion signal, C-terminal His8 tag, and T4 fibritin foldon in pCDNA3.4 for trimer stabilisation, (2) transfection conditions and kifunensine treatment for homogeneous glycosylation, (3) Ni-NTA affinity purification from conditioned medium, (4) SEC-purification on Superdex 200 to isolate trimer fraction, (5) QC: SDS-PAGE, BN-PAGE for trimer confirmation, ACE2-Fc binding ELISA, (6) endotoxin removal by Triton X-114 phase separation. Include a note on biosafety classification: recombinant RBD is BSL-1 material.
source_citation: Derived from uniprot (2024_05) and published SARS-CoV-2 antigen production protocols; target: SARS-CoV-2 spike RBD; organism: SARS-CoV-2; catalogue version v1.0

_provenance.target_class: SARS-CoV-2 receptor-binding domain for vaccine and diagnostic applications
